steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question asking about the relationship between Kenny and Lysyanskaya. The passage's final sentence is the decisive clue: "Kenny and others like her helped make it possible for more women — such as Anna Lysyanskaya — to enter the field of cryptology." Kenny is an early contributor; Lysyanskaya is a current practitioner in the same field.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Minnie McNeal Kenny taught Anna Lysyanskaya how to securely manage important data.’: The passage never says Kenny taught Lysyanskaya directly. Kenny's contributions were administrative and award-winning, not described as personal instruction to any individual. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Minnie McNeal Kenny and Anna Lysyanskaya worked together early in their careers.’: The passage gives no indication their careers overlapped in time; Kenny is presented as an earlier pioneer and Lysyanskaya as a current practitioner. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Minnie McNeal Kenny founded an organization that Anna Lysyanskaya currently works for.’: The passage names the NSA and the National Cryptologic School as organizations Kenny worked within, not ones she founded — and nothing links Lysyanskaya to either institution. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Minnie McNeal Kenny made earlier contributions to a field that Anna Lysyanskaya currently works within.’: This matches the passage exactly: Kenny's earlier contributions helped open the field of cryptology, and Lysyanskaya currently works and teaches within it. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
